summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on two zoning cases. The Commission administered the oath of office to new member Tammy Purcell. They approved a zone change from M1 to PDM for PLK Communities' proposed 274-unit multifamily development on Smith Road with stipulations requiring a traffic study, parking density corrections, and unit density compliance. They also reviewed the final development plan for Woodhall Crossing Section 2, a 31-lot single-family residential phase.
decisions: Approved ZC-2025-001 zone change from M1 to PDM for Parcel 112208B127 with stipulations requiring traffic study, addressing parking space density, and addressing unit density issues per Chapter 6 (unanimous vote)
action_items: PLK Communities to provide traffic impact study and address ODOT requirements before preliminary plan submission
PLK Communities to resolve unit density issue (currently 9.528 units/acre vs. 8 units/acre maximum allowed)
PLK Communities to resolve parking space shortfall (417 provided vs. 548 required at 2 stalls per unit)
PLK Communities to address jurisdictional wetland delineation findings affecting building footprints
Woodhall Crossing developer to apply for ODOT traffic signal as part of Section 3 submittal (required at 85 lots)
key_amounts: 274 apartment and townhome units proposed by PLK Communities
28.77 acres total site area for PLK Communities project
417 total parking stalls provided vs. 548 required (2 per unit)
9.528 units per acre proposed vs. 8 units per acre maximum allowed
65% total open space proposed (25% minimum required)
31 buildable lots on 10.38 acres for Woodhall Crossing Section 2
225 total buildable lots for entire Woodhall Crossing development
PLK Communities employs over 240 full-time employees and has nearly 8,000 units
7,625 square foot minimum lot area for Woodhall Crossing
notable_topics: Oath of office for new Zoning Commission member Tammy Purcell
Zone change from M1 to PDM for PLK Communities multifamily development at 6300 Smith Road
Concerns about traffic congestion on State Route 28 and surrounding roads
Discussion about rental vs. condominium ownership model
Concern about changing zoning before preliminary plan approval (cart before the horse)
Final development plan for Woodhall Crossing Section 2 single-family residential
ODOT traffic signal requirements and traffic impact studies
Woodhall Crossing architectural standards and phasing schedule
attendees: Mr. Dinwiddie (Zoning Commission member - absent/no response at roll call)
Tammy Purcell (new Zoning Commission member, sworn in)
notes: Mr. Dinwiddie appears to have been absent as no response was recorded at roll call. The transcript for the Woodhall Crossing case (ZC-2025-002) appears to have been cut off due to a recording/transcription error with extensive repetition at the end, so no vote on that case was captured. PLK Communities indicated they plan to close on the property purchase by end of June 2025 with the zone change being contingent on the sale. The Commission chose to combine the zone change with preliminary plan approval requirements as stipulations rather than approving them separately.
title: ZC 6-3-25
